components.name: U307
components.inferred_role: Inverter (single NOT gate). Provides logical inversion of a signal elsewhere in the system; its EN pin is used as a control input for enabling/disabling the gate.
components.justification: Part number 74AHC1G04 is documented as a single inverter with an active‑low enable pin on pin 5 (see TI Little Logic Guide, AHC family). The netlist connects only pin 5, confirming the enable function is being used.

components.name: U309
components.inferred_role: 3‑input OR gate. Combines three separate logic signals into one; its EN pin is also used as a control input.
components.justification: Part number 74LVC1G332 is listed in the Little Logic Guide as a single 3‑input OR gate with an enable pin on pin 5. The netlist shows only pin 5 connected, indicating the enable line is the relevant function here.

connections.justification: Pin 5 on both devices is the ENABLE (active‑low) pin. The net name "/BAT_SW" (active‑low battery‑switch control) together with the enable function identifies this as a control signal.

relationships.description: Both logic devices share the same enable control; their operation is dependent on the state of the /BAT_SW signal.
relationships.justification: The only connection present is the shared ENABLE pin, meaning each gate’s output is gated by the same control line.

safety_concerns.issue: Improper level on /BAT_SW could unintentionally disable the inverter and OR gate, leaving downstream control signals undefined.
safety_concerns.reason: EN pins are active‑low; a high level (or noise that keeps the line high) puts the gates in high‑impedance mode. If these gates drive a battery‑switch control circuit, the battery may remain enabled when it should be disabled, creating a safety hazard.

safety_concerns.issue: If /BAT_SW is driven above the devices' supply voltage (VCC), the enable pins could be overstressed.
safety_concerns.reason: Both IC families tolerate input voltages up to VCC+0.5 V (AHC up to 5.5 V, LVC up to 5.5 V). Assuming VCC is within the specified range, normal operation is safe, but an external fault that forces a higher voltage could exceed the rating.
